steps.title: Identify the diameter and what we're finding
steps.content: QS is a diameter of the circle with center C, and its length is 76 mm. Point R lies on the circle, so segment CR connects the center to a point on the circle — meaning CR is a radius of the circle.

steps.title: Apply the diameter-radius relationship
steps.content:
For any circle, the radius is half the diameter. Since QS is the diameter:
$$CR = \frac{QS}{2} = \frac{76}{2} = 38 \text{ mm}$$
That is the correct answer.
